summary:
The provided information indicates that glandular cell abnormalities like atypical glandular cells (AGCs) are uncommon and typically require careful evaluation due to their rarity and potential significance. Although liquid-based cytology improves detection capabilities for such findings compared to traditional methods, existing technologies used in rapid prescreening—such as automated systems—are mainly optimized for squamous abnormalities and do not have established reliability in detecting glandular pathologies directly. Thus, based solely on documented evidence, rapid prescreening fails to consistently or reliably detect glandular cell abnormalities.

Only include content related to the core medical facts presented above. Do NOT add external knowledge or commentary beyond those stated in the original text.
